Idiomatic Expressions

  • 楚州界有小山 - Literal: 'There is a small mountain in the jurisdiction of Chu Prefecture.' Introduces the setting.
  • 山上有室而无水 - Literal: 'There is a room on the mountain but no water.' Sets up the contrast between the room and the lack of water.
  • 僧智一掘井 - Literal: 'Monk Zhiyi dug a well.' Introduces the main character and action.
  • 深三丈遇石 - Literal: 'After digging three zhang (a unit of length, approximately 3 meters), he encountered a stone.' Describes the initial obstacle.
  • 凿石穴及土，又深五十尺 - Literal: 'After chiseling a hole in the stone and reaching the soil, he dug another fifty feet.' Continues the description of the digging process.
  • 得一玉，长尺二，阔四尺 - Literal: 'He found a piece of jade, one foot two inches long and four feet wide.' Describes the size of the jade.
  • 赤如□□，每面有六龟子 - Literal: 'Red like □□, with six turtle patterns on each side.' The □□ is likely a placeholder for a missing or unclear character, possibly '火' (fire) or '血' (blood).
  • 中若可贮水状 - Literal: 'Inside it appears as if it could hold water.' Describes the appearance of the jade.
  • 僧偶击一角视之，遂沥血 - Literal: 'The monk accidentally struck one corner to examine it, and it started to bleed.' Describes an unusual reaction.
  • 半月日方止 - Literal: 'It stopped after half a month.' Indicates the duration of the bleeding.
  • 虞乡有山观 - Literal: 'In Shu County, there is a mountain pavilion.' Introduces a new setting.
  • 甚幽寂 - Literal: 'Very secluded and quiet.' Describes the atmosphere of the pavilion.
  • 有涤阳道士居焉 - Literal: 'There lives a Taoist priest named Deyang.' Introduces the main character of the second part.
  • 太和中，道士尝一夕独登坛 - Literal: 'During the Taihe period, the Taoist priest once climbed the altar alone one night.' Sets the scene for the event.
  • 望见庭□忽有异光，自井泉中发 - Literal: 'He saw a strange light suddenly appear in the courtyard, coming from the well spring.' The □ is likely a placeholder for a missing character, possibly '庭' (courtyard).
  • 俄有一物，状若兔，其色若精金 - Literal: 'Soon, something appeared, shaped like a rabbit, with a color like pure gold.' Describes the appearance of the object.
  • 随光而出，环绕醮坛 - Literal: 'Following the light, it emerged and circled the altar.' Describes the movement of the object.
  • 久之，复入于井 - Literal: 'After a while, it returned to the well.' Describes the final movement of the object.
  • 自是每夕辄见 - Literal: 'From then on, he saw it every night.' Indicates the regularity of the sightings.
  • 不敢告于人 - Literal: 'He dared not tell anyone.' Describes the priest's reaction.
  • 后因淘井得一金兔 - Literal: 'Later, when he cleaned the well, he found a golden rabbit.' Describes the discovery of the rabbit.
  • 甚小，奇光烂然 - Literal: 'Very small, with a wondrous and brilliant light.' Describes the appearance of the rabbit.
  • 即置于巾箱中 - Literal: 'He immediately placed it in his hairbox.' Describes the priest's action.
  • 时御史李戎职于蒲津 - Literal: 'At that time, the Censorate official Li Rong served in Puzhen.' Introduces a new character.
  • 与道士友善，道士因以遗之 - Literal: 'He was friends with the Taoist priest, who then gave it to him.' Describes the transfer of the rabbit.
  • 其后戎自奉先县令为忻州刺史 - Literal: 'Later, Li Rong was promoted from the magistrate of Fengxian County to the governor of Xinzhou.' Describes Li Rong's career advancement.
  • 其金兔忽亡去 - Literal: 'His golden rabbit suddenly disappeared.' Describes the disappearance of the rabbit.
  • 后月余而戎卒 - Literal: 'After more than a month, Li Rong died.' Describes the outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楚州 - Refers to Chu Prefecture, an administrative division in ancient China.
  • 僧智一 - The name of the monk who dug the well.

  • 车，古蒌也，蒌似屏 - Means 'the carriage is called 'lou,' which resembles a screen.'
  • 世人死者有作伎乐，名为乐丧 - Means 'people make musical performances for the deceased, called 'music for the dead.'
  • 头，所以存亡者之魂气也 - Means 'to protect the spirit of the deceased.'
  • 一名苏衣被，苏苏如也 - Means 'also known as 'soothing the spirit,' which is a gentle and comforting act.'
  • 一曰狂阻，一曰触圹 - Means 'also called 'wild obstruction' or 'touching the grave.'
  • 四目曰方相，两目曰亻欺 - Means 'a four-eyed creature is called 'fangxiang,' and a two-eyed creature is called 'renqi.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亡人坐上作魂衣，謂之上天衣 - The deceased sits on a chair and makes a soul robe, which is called the 'heavenly robe.'
  • 送亡者不镜赍奁盖 - When sending off the deceased, one does not bring a mirror, a box, or a lid.
  • [B168]，鬼衣也 - [B168] is a ghost robe.
  • 桐人起虞卿，明衣起左伯桃，挽歌起绋讴 - The桐人 (Tong people) raised虞卿 (Yu Qian), the明衣 (bright clothing) raised左伯桃 (Zuo Bota), and the挽歌 (funeral song) arose from绋讴 (funeral chants).
  • 故旧律发冢弃市，冢者重也，言为孝子所重，发一{尔虫}土则坐，不须物也 - According to the old law, one who opens a tomb is executed in the marketplace. The tomb is heavy, meaning it is valued by filial sons. If one disturbs even a small amount of earth, they are punished, without needing any additional evidence.
  • “吊”字，矢贯弓也 - The character '吊' means an arrow passing through a bow.
  • 古者葬弃中野，《礼》：贯弓而吊，以助鸟兽之害 - In ancient times, burials were left in the wilderness. According to the《礼》 (Rites), one would pass an arrow through a bow to assist birds and animals in harming the body.
  • 后魏俗竟厚葬，棺厚高大，多用柏木，两边作大铜钮，不问公私贵贱，悉白油络帏车，素槊仗，打虏鼓，哭声欲似南朝 - In the later Wei dynasty, there was a trend of elaborate burials. The coffins were thick and large, often made of cypress wood, with large copper handles on both sides. Regardless of public or private status, the coffins were adorned with white oil, and the funeral carriages were decorated with white silk and white spears. Drums were beaten, and the cries sounded like those in the Southern Dynasties.
  • 传哭挽歌无破声，亦小异于京师焉 - The funeral songs and cries had no breaking sounds, but they were slightly different from those in the capital.
  • 《周礼》：“方相氏区攵罔象。”罔象好食亡者肝，而畏虎与柏 - According to《周礼》 (Rites of Zhou), 'the Fangxiangshi (a ritual official) deals with the wu (spirits).' The wu like to eat the liver of the deceased and fear tigers and cypress trees.
  • 墓上树柏，路口致石虎，为此也 - Cypress trees are planted on tombs, and stone tigers are placed at the entrance, for this reason.
  • 昔秦时陈仓人，猎得兽若彘而不知名 - In the past, during the Qin dynasty, a man from Chen Cang caught an animal resembling a pig but did not know its name.
  • 道逢二童子，曰：“此名弗述，常在地中食死人脑。欲杀之，当以柏插其首” - He met two children who said, 'This is called Fu Shu, and it often lives in the ground, eating the brains of the dead. To kill it, one should insert a cypress branch into its head.'
  • 遭丧妇人有面衣，期已下妇人着帼，不着面衣 - A woman who has experienced a death wears a face mask, while a woman who has completed the mourning period wears a headdress and does not wear a face mask.
  • 又妇人哭，以扇掩面 - A woman also cries, covering her face with a fan.
  • 或有帷幄内哭者 - Some women cry inside a tent.

  • 地陷 - Literally means 'ground collapses,' often used metaphorically to describe unexpected discoveries or events.
  • 衤昆五十腰 - A phrase that is difficult to translate directly. '衤昆' (kun) is an archaic form of '昆' (kun), which can mean 'brother' or 'relative.' '五十腰' (wushizao) likely refers to a type of garment or clothing, possibly a robe or a belt. The exact meaning is uncertain, but it seems to describe a significant find within the tomb.
  • 處士鄭賓于言 - '處士' (chushi) means 'a scholar who does not hold an official position.' '鄭賓于' (Zheng Bin Yu) is the name of the person speaking.
  • 嘗客河北 - '嘗' (chang) means 'once' or 'formerly,' and '客' (ke) means 'to visit' or 'to be a guest.' '河北' (Hebei) refers to the region north of the Yellow River.
  • 日暮 - Literally means 'sunset' or 'evening.'
  • 樂聲漸近 - Means 'music sounds approaching.'
  • 及入房 - Means 'as it enters the room.'
  • 如在梁栋间 - Means 'as if in the beams and rafters,' suggesting the music is coming from above.
  • 旋出门 - Means 'turning out the door.'
  • 随乐声而去 - Means 'following the music away.'
  • 一更 - Refers to the first watch of the night, typically around 7 PM.
  • 村正方归 - '村正' (cun zheng) means 'village headman' or 'village chief.' '方归' (fang gui) means 'just returned.'
  • 被酒大骂 - '被酒' (bei jiu) means 'drunk,' and '大骂' (da ma) means 'shout loudly' or 'scold loudly.'
  • 折一桑枝如臂 - '折' (zhe) means 'to break' or 'to snap,' '桑枝' (sang zhi) means 'mulberry branch,' and '如臂' (ru bi) means 'as long as an arm.'
  • 遂负尸而返 - '遂' (sui) means 'then' or 'thereupon,' '负' (fu) means 'to carry on the back,' and '而返' (er fan) means 'and return.'
  • 医僧行儒说 - '医僧行儒' (yi xing ru) is the name of the monk who spoke.
  • 福州有弘济上人 - '福州' (Fuzhou) is a city in Fujian Province, '有' (you) means 'has,' and '弘济上人' (Hongji Shangren) is the name of the monk.
  • 斋戒清苦 - '斋戒' (zhai jie) means 'fasting and abstinence,' and '清苦' (qing ku) means 'simple and austere.'
  • 常于沙岸得一颅骨 - '常' (chang) means 'often,' '于' (yu) means 'at,' '沙岸' (sha an) means 'sandbank,' '得' (de) means 'to obtain,' and '一颅骨' (yi lu gu) means 'one skull.'
  • 遂贮衣篮中归寺 - '遂' (sui) means 'then,' '贮' (zhu) means 'to store,' '衣篮' (yi lan) means 'clothing basket,' '中' (zhong) means 'inside,' and '归寺' (gui si) means 'return to the temple.'
  • 月黑 - Means 'dark moon' or 'no moonlight.'
  • 不敢寻逐 - '不敢' (bu gan) means 'dare not,' and '寻逐' (xun zhu) means 'to search and pursue.'
  • 复闻乐声在一柏林上 - '复' (fu) means 'again,' '闻' (wen) means 'to hear,' '乐声' (le sheng) means 'music sound,' '一柏林' (yi bo lin) means 'one pine forest,' and '上' (shang) means 'above.'
  • 及近树 - '及' (ji) means 'when,' '近' (jin) means 'near,' and '树' (shu) means 'tree.'
  • 树下有火荧荧然 - '树下' (shu xia) means 'under the tree,' '有' (you) means 'there is,' '火' (huo) means 'fire,' and '荧荧然' (ying ying ran) means 'flickering.'
  • 烛之 - '烛' (zhu) means 'to illuminate' or 'to light,' and '之' (zhi) means 'it' or 'itself.'
  • 责曰 - '责' (ze) means 'to rebuke,' and '曰' (yue) means 'said.'
  • 尔不能求生人天，凭朽骨何也 - '尔' (er) means 'you,' '不能' (bu neng) means 'cannot,' '求' (qiu) means 'seek,' '生人天' (sheng ren tian) means 'to be reborn in heaven,' '凭' (ping) means 'rely on,' '朽骨' (xiu gu) means 'rotten bones,' and '何也' (he ye) means 'why.'

  • 帝断其首，葬之常羊山 - Narrates the emperor's punishment of Xing Tian by cutting off his head and burying it in Changyang Mountain.
  • 乃以乱为目，脐为口，操干戚而舞焉 - Describes Xing Tian's transformation, with his confusion as eyes, navel as a mouth, and wielding axes and shields in dance, symbolizing his eternal struggle.
  • 天神下若流火 - Depicts the descent of a heavenly deity as if flowing fire, using vivid imagery.
  • 天翁姓张名坚，字刺渴，渔阳人 - Introduces a deity, '天翁' (Lord of Heaven), with a name, courtesy name, and place of origin, using classical biographical style.
  • 少不羁，无所拘忌 - Describes the deity's youthful character as unrestrained and without fear of restrictions.
  • 常张罗得一白雀，爱而养之 - Narrates the deity's act of catching and raising a white pheasant, emphasizing his compassion.
  • 梦天刘翁责怒，每欲杀之，白雀辄以报坚，坚设诸方待之，终莫能害 - Describes a dream where the deity faces punishment from '刘翁' (Liu翁), with the white pheasant warning him, and his attempts to avoid harm.
  • 天翁遂下观之，坚盛设宾主，乃窃骑天翁车，乘白龙，振策登天 - Narrates the deity's descent to observe, followed by his theft of the Lord of Heaven's chariot, riding a white dragon, and ascending to heaven.
  • 天公乘余龙追之，不及 - Describes the Lord of Heaven's pursuit and failure to catch the deity.
  • 坚既到玄宫，易百官，杜塞北门，封白雀为上卿侯，改白雀之胤不产于下土 - Narrates the deity's arrival in the celestial palace, replacing officials, sealing the northern gate, and granting the white pheasant a noble title, preventing its descendants from being born on earth.
  • 刘翁失治，徘徊五岳作灾 - Describes the Lord of Heaven's loss of control and his wandering among the Five Sacred Mountains, causing calamities.
  • 坚患之，以刘翁为太山太守，主生死之籍 - Narrates the deity's solution to the problem by appointing the Lord of Heaven as the governor of Mount Tai, overseeing the records of life and death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 浑氵敦 - A mythical deity associated with the Tianshan Mountains, known for its unusual form and connection to music and dance.
  • 帝江 - A significant figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with the sky and weather.
  • 形夭 - A mythological figure who challenged the emperor and was punished by having his head cut off and buried in Changyang Mountain.
  • 汉竹宫 - A ritual site from the Han dynasty, used for important ceremonies.
  • 太一君 - A deity in Chinese mythology, often associated with the supreme deity and the heavens.
  • 天翁 - The Lord of Heaven, a central figure in Chinese cosmology, often depicted as the ruler of the celestial realm.
  • 五岳 - The Five Sacred Mountains in Chinese mythology, each associated with a cardinal direction and a specific deity.
  • 太山太守 - The governor of Mount Tai, a significant position in Chinese mythology and governance, responsible for overseeing the records of life and death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北斗魁 - The handle of the Big Dipper constellation, a significant astronomical and mythological symbol in Chinese culture.
  • 神名执（一曰报）阴 - The name of the first star in the Big Dipper, '执' (zhí) or '报' (bào), meaning 'hold' or 'report,' respectively, with the additional note '阴' (yīn), meaning 'yin' or 'negative.'
  • 叶诣 - The name of the second star, '叶诣' (yè yì), which can be interpreted as 'leaf arrival' or 'leaf journey.'
  • 视金 - The name of the third star, '视金' (shì jīn), which can be interpreted as 'gold view' or 'gold sight.'
  • 拒理 - The name of the fourth star, '拒理' (jù lǐ), which can be interpreted as 'resist reason' or 'oppose logic.'
  • 防作 - The name of the fifth star, '防作' (fáng zuò), which can be interpreted as 'prevent action' or 'guard against doing.'
  • 开宝 - The name of the sixth star, '开宝' (kāi bǎo), which can be interpreted as 'open treasure' or 'reveal treasure.'
  • 招摇（一曰始） - The name of the seventh star, '招摇' (zhāo yáo) or '始' (shǐ), meaning 'attract' or 'beginning,' respectively.
  • 东王公讳倪，字君明 - '东王公' (Dōng Wánggōng) is a deity in Chinese mythology, '讳倪' (huì ní) means 'surname Ni,' and '字君明' (zì Jūnmíng) means 'courtesy name Junming.'
  • 天下未有人民时，秩二万六千石 - '天下未有人民时' (tiānxià wèi yǒu rénmín shí) means 'when there were no people in the world,' and '秩二万六千石' (zhì èr wàn liù qiān dàn) refers to a high rank in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 佩杂绶，绶长六丈六尺 - '佩杂绶' (pèi zá shòu) means 'wear a mixed ribbon,' and '绶长六丈六尺' (shòu cháng liù zhàng liù chǐ) means 'the ribbon is six zhang and six chi long.'
  • 从女九千 - '从女九千' (cóng nǚ jiǔ qiān) means 'followed by nine thousand women.'
  • 以丁亥日死 - '以丁亥日死' (yǐ dīng hài rì sǐ) means 'died on the day of Ding Hai.'
  • 西王母姓杨，讳回，治昆仑西北隅 - '西王母' (Xī Wángmǔ) is another deity in Chinese mythology, '姓杨' (xìng Yáng) means 'surname Yang,' '讳回' (huì huí) means 'surname Hui,' and '治昆仑西北隅' (zhì Kūnlún xīběi yú) means 'governs the northwest corner of Mount Kunlun.'
  • 以丁丑日死 - '以丁丑日死' (yǐ dīng chǒu rì sǐ) means 'died on the day of Ding Chou.'
  • 灶神名隗，状如美女 - '灶神' (zào shén) is the god of the kitchen stove, '名隗' (míng Kuí) means 'named Kuí,' and '状如美女' (zhuàng rú měinǚ) means 'looks like a beautiful woman.'
  • 又姓张名单，字子郭 - '又姓张名单' (yòu xìng Zhāng dān) means 'also surnamed Zhang and named Dan,' and '字子郭' (zì Zǐguō) means 'courtesy name Ziguó.'
  • 夫人字卿忌，有六女，皆名察（一作祭）洽 - '夫人字卿忌' (fūrén zì Qīngjì) means 'wife with the courtesy name Qingji,' '有六女' (yǒu liù nǚ) means 'has six daughters,' and '皆名察（一作祭）洽' (jiē míng chá (yī zuò jì) qià) means 'all named Chá (or Jì) Qià.'
  • 常以月晦日上天白人罪状 - '常以月晦日上天白人罪状' (cháng yǐ yuè huì rì shàngtiān báirén zuìzhuàng) means 'often on the last day of the month, ascends to heaven to report people's sins.'
  • 大者夺纪，纪三百日，小者夺算，算一百日 - '大者夺纪，纪三百日' (dà zhě duó jì, jì sān bǎi rì) means 'for major sins, lose a jì (a period of 300 days),' and '小者夺算，算一百日' (xiǎo zhě duó suàn, suàn yī bǎi rì) means 'for minor sins, lose a suàn (a period of 100 days).'
  • 故为天帝督使，下为地精 - '故为天帝督使' (gù wèi tiāndì dūshǐ) means 'therefore, as a supervisor of the Heavenly Emperor,' and '下为地精' (xià wèi dījīng) means 'on earth, as a terrestrial spirit.'
  • 己丑日，日出卯时上天，禺中下行署，此日祭得福 - '己丑日，日出卯时上天' (jǐ chǒu rì, rìchū mǎo shí shàngtiān) means 'on the day of Jǐ Chǒu, ascends to heaven at the hour of mao (dawn),' '禺中下行署' (yú zhōng xiàxíng shǔ) means 'descends to the lower realms during the midday,' and '此日祭得福' (cǐ rì jì dé fú) means 'on this day, sacrifice brings good fortune.'
  • 其属神有天帝娇孙、天帝大夫、天帝都尉、天帝长兄、硎上童子、突上紫宫君、太和君、玉池夫人等 - '其属神有天帝娇孙、天帝大夫、天帝都尉、天帝长兄、硎上童子、突上紫宫君、太和君、玉池夫人等' (qí shǔ shén yǒu tiāndì jiāo sūn, tiāndì dàfū, tiāndì dūwèi, tiāndì zhǎng xiōng, xíng shàng tóngzǐ, tū shàng zǐgōng jūn, tài hé jūn, yù chí fūrén děng) means 'its associated deities include the Heavenly Emperor's beloved grandson, the Heavenly Emperor's minister, the Heavenly Emperor's general, the Heavenly Emperor's eldest brother, the child on the grindstone, the purple palace lord, the Great Harmony Lord, and the Jade Pool Lady, etc.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北斗魁 - The Big Dipper constellation is a significant astronomical and mythological symbol in Chinese culture, often associated with guidance and navigation.
  • 东王公 - A male deity in Chinese mythology, often paired with the Western Queen Mother (西王母).
  • 西王母 - A female deity in Chinese mythology, often associated with immortality and longevity.
  • 灶神 - The god of the kitchen stove, responsible for household prosperity and well-being.
  • 天帝 - The Heavenly Emperor, the supreme deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 天帝娇孙、天帝大夫、天帝都尉、天帝长兄、硎上童子、突上紫宫君、太和君、玉池夫人等 - Various deities associated with the Heavenly Emperor, each with specific roles and attributes.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 北斗魁第一星神名执（一曰报）阴，第二星曰叶诣，第三星曰视金，第四星曰拒理，第五星曰防作，第六星曰开宝，第七星曰招摇（一曰始） - This long sequence must be read as a list of the seven stars in the Big Dipper, each with a specific name and meaning.
  • 东王公讳倪，字君明。天下未有人民时，秩二万六千石。佩杂绶，绶长六丈六尺。从女九千。以丁亥日死。 - This sequence describes the attributes and actions of the Eastern Lord, including his rank, attire, followers, and date of death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 河伯 - Refers to the River God, a figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 人面，乘兩龍 - Describes the River God as having a human face and riding two dragons.
  • 一曰冰夷，一曰冯夷 - Alternative names for the River God, indicating multiple traditions or interpretations.
  • 人面鱼身 - Another description of the River God, with a human face and fish body.
  • 《金匮》言名冯循 - Refers to a text (*Jin Gui*) that mentions the name 'Feng Xun' for the River God.
  • 《河图》言姓吕名夷 - Refers to a text (*He Tu*) that mentions the River God's surname as 'Lv' and his given name as 'Yi.'
  • 《穆天子传》言无夷 - Refers to a text (*Mu Tianzi Zhuan*) that mentions the River God as 'Wu Yi.'
  • 《淮南子》言冯迟 - Refers to a text (*Huainanzi*) that mentions the River God as 'Feng Chi.'
  • 甲子神名弓隆 - Refers to a deity associated with the *Jiazi* day, named 'Gong Long.'
  • 甲戍神名执明 - Refers to a deity associated with the *Jia Xu* day, named 'Zhi Ming.'
  • 《太真科经》说 - Refers to a text (*Tai Zhen Ke Jing*) that mentions various spirits and rituals.
  • 鬼仙 - Refers to a type of spirit or ghost.
  • 丙戌日鬼名{龙}生 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Bing Xu* day, named 'Sheng.'
  • 丙午日鬼名挺农 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Bing Wu* day, named 'Ting Nong.'
  • 乙卯日鬼名天陪 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Yi Mao* day, named 'Tian Pei.'
  • 戊午日鬼名耳述 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Wu Wu* day, named 'Er Shu.'
  • 壬戌日鬼名<辶春> - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Ren Xu* day, named 'Chun.'
  • 辛丑日鬼名<辶氏> - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Xin Chou* day, named 'Shi.'
  • 乙酉日鬼名聂左 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Yi You* day, named 'Nie Zuo.'
  • 丙辰日鬼名夭雄 - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Bing Chen* day, named 'Yao Xiong.'
  • 辛卯日鬼名{棘心} - Refers to a spirit associated with the *Xin Mao* day, named 'Ji Xin.'
  • 酉虫鬼名发廷<辶毛> - Refers to a spirit associated with the *You* day, named 'Fa Ting Mao.'
  • 厕鬼名顼天竺 - Refers to a spirit associated with the toilet, named 'Xu Tian Zhu.'
  • 语忘、敬遗，二鬼名 - Refers to two spirits named 'Yu Wang' and 'Jing Yi.'
  • 妇人临产呼之，不害人 - Refers to a ritual where women call upon these spirits during childbirth to avoid harm.
  • 长三寸三分，上下乌衣 - Describes the physical appearance of the spirits.
  • 马鬼名赐 - Refers to a spirit associated with horses, named 'Ci.'
  • 蛇鬼名侠刂石圭 - Refers to a spirit associated with snakes, named 'Xie Shi Gui.'
  • 井鬼名琼 - Refers to a spirit associated with wells, named 'Qiong.'
  • 衣服鬼名甚僚 - Refers to a spirit associated with clothing, named 'Shen Liao.'
  • 神荼、郁垒领万鬼 - Refers to two deities, Shen Tu and Yu Lei, who lead all spirits.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河伯 - The River God is a significant figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with water and fertility.
  • 八石 - Refers to a type of elixir or potion used in alchemical practices for achieving immortality.
  • 八月上庚日 - A specific date in the lunar calendar, often associated with rituals and ceremonies.
  • 神荼、郁垒 - These are protective deities in Chinese mythology, often depicted as leading all spirits.

  • 河伯 - The River God.
  • 无夷 - Another name for the River God.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有神异 - Means 'possesses supernatural abilities' or 'is endowed with mystical powers.'
  • 力能降伏毒龙 - Means 'has the power to subdue venomous dragons.' '降伏' can be translated as 'subdue' or 'overcome.'
  • 贾人买市人金银宝货 - Refers to 'merchants buying gold and silver treasures from the market.' '贾人' means 'merchant,' and '市人' means 'market people' or 'people in the market.'
  • 至夜中，钱并化为炭 - Means 'by night, the money all turned into charcoal.' '化为' can be translated as 'turned into.'
  • 境内数百家皆失金宝 - Means 'within the kingdom, hundreds of households lost their gold and treasures.' '境内' means 'within the kingdom' or 'in the territory.'
  • 王有男先出家，成阿罗汉果 - Means 'the king had a son who first entered the monastic life and attained the state of Arhat.' '出家' means 'entered the monastic life,' and '阿罗汉' is a term for a high level of spiritual attainment in Buddhism.
  • 王问之 - Means 'the king asked him.'
  • 罗汉曰 - Means 'the Arhat said.'
  • 此龙所为 - Means 'this was done by the dragon.'
  • 龙居北山 - Means 'the dragon lives in the north mountain.'
  • 其头若虎 - Means 'its head is like a tiger.'
  • 今在某处眠耳 - Means 'it is now sleeping somewhere.'
  • 王乃易衣持剑默出 - Means 'the king then changed his clothes, held a sword, and silently left.' '易衣' means 'changed his clothes,' and '默出' means 'silently left.'
  • 至龙所 - Means 'arrived at the dragon's place.'
  • 见龙卧，将欲斩之 - Means 'saw the dragon lying down and was about to cut it off.' '斩之' means 'cut it off' or 'kill it.'
  • 因曰 - Means 'then said.'
  • 吾斩寐龙，谁知吾有神力 - Means 'I am cutting a sleeping dragon; who would know I have supernatural strength?' '寐龙' means 'sleeping dragon.'
  • 遂叱龙 - Means 'then shouted at the dragon.' '叱' means 'shout' or 'rebuke.'
  • 龙惊起，化为狮子 - Means 'the dragon was startled and woke up, transforming into a lion.' '惊起' means 'startled and woke up,' and '化为' means 'transformed into.'
  • 王即乘其上 - Means 'the king immediately mounted it.' '乘其上' means 'mounted it.'
  • 龙怒，作雷声 - Means 'the dragon became angry and made thunderous sounds.' '作雷声' means 'made thunderous sounds.'
  • 腾空至城北二十里 - Means 'flew through the air to twenty miles north of the city.' '腾空' means 'flew through the air,' and '二十里' means 'twenty miles.'
  • 尔不降，当断尔头 - Means 'if you do not submit, I will cut off your head.' '尔不降' means 'if you do not submit,' and '当断尔头' means 'I will cut off your head.'
  • 龙惧王神力 - Means 'the dragon feared the king's supernatural strength.'
  • 乃作人语曰 - Means 'then spoke in human language and said.'
  • 勿杀我，我当与王乘，欲有所向，随心即至 - Means 'do not kill me; I will accompany the king, and wherever he wishes to go, it will be there immediately.' '勿杀我' means 'do not kill me,' '我当与王乘' means 'I will accompany the king,' and '欲有所向，随心即至' means 'wherever he wishes to go, it will be there immediately.'
  • 王许之 - Means 'the king agreed.'
  • 后常乘龙而行 - Means 'later, he often rode the dragon to travel.' '后常乘龙而行' means 'later, he often rode the dragon to travel.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 古龟兹国王阿主儿者 - Refers to an ancient king of the kingdom of Kucha named A Zuer. Kucha was an important kingdom in the Tarim Basin during the Han and Tang dynasties.
  • 阿罗汉 - A term for a high level of spiritual attainment in Buddhism, specifically the fourth and final stage of enlightenment.
  • 龙 - In Chinese culture, dragons are powerful and revered creatures, often associated with water and weather. They are also symbols of imperial power and good fortune.
  • 狮子 - Lions are often associated with strength and courage in Chinese culture, particularly in Buddhist iconography.
  • 城北二十里 - Refers to a specific distance north of the city, which can be translated as 'twenty miles north of the city.'

Proper Nouns

  • 古龟兹国王阿主儿者 - The ancient king of the kingdom of Kucha named A Zuer.
  • 阿罗汉 - A high level of spiritual attainment in Buddhism.
  • 龙 - A powerful and revered creature in Chinese culture.
  • 狮子 - A symbol of strength and courage in Chinese culture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干陀国 - Refers to the country of Gandhara, an ancient kingdom in the northwestern region of South Asia.
  • 讨袭诸国 - Means 'to attack and conquer various countries.'
  • 所向悉降 - Means 'all submitted to him.'
  • 五天竺国 - Refers to the five regions of India, often associated with the five major kingdoms or regions of ancient India.
  • 得上细<系>二条 - Means 'he obtained two fine silk scarves.'
  • 自留一 - Means 'he kept one for himself.'
  • 一与妃 - Means 'he gave one to his queen.'
  • <系>当妃乳上有郁金香手印迹 - Means 'the scarf had a handprint of saffron on the queen's breast.'
  • 王见惊恐 - Means 'the king was shocked and frightened.'
  • 尔忽着此手迹之服，何也 - Means 'Why did you suddenly wear this garment with a handprint?'
  • 向王所赐之<系> - Means 'the scarf that the king gave me.'
  • 王怒问藏臣 - Means 'the king angrily questioned the keeper of the treasury.'
  • <系>本有是，非臣之咎 - Means 'The scarf originally had this, it is not the fault of the servant.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 干陀国 - Gandhara was an ancient kingdom in the northwestern region of South Asia, known for its rich cultural and artistic heritage.
  • 讨袭诸国 - This phrase reflects the military campaigns and conquests typical of ancient rulers.
  • 五天竺国 - The five regions of India were significant in ancient Indian history and culture, often associated with the five major kingdoms or regions.
  • <系> - Refers to a type of silk scarf or sash, often used as a symbol of status or gift-giving.
  • 郁金香手印迹 - The handprint of saffron is a significant symbol in Buddhist and Hindu traditions, often associated with divine or royal presence.

Proper Nouns

  • 干陀国 - The country of Gandhara.
  • 伽当 - The name of the king, also known as '加色伽当.'
  • 五天竺国 - The five regions of India.
  • <系> - A type of silk scarf or sash.
  • 郁金香手印迹 - The handprint of saffron.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王追商者问之 - The king followed the merchant and questioned him.
  • 有宿愿 - Has a long-standing wish.
  • 吾若不以剑裁娑陀婆恨王手足，无以寝食 - If I do not cut off the hands and feet of King Sathapaha with my sword, I cannot eat or sleep.
  • 乃遣使就南天竺索娑陀婆恨王手足 - He then sent an envoy to South India to request the hands and feet of King Sathapaha.
  • 王知其伪，且自恃福力，因断金人手足，娑陀婆恨王于窟中，手足亦自落也 - The king knew it was a deception, and relying on his own power, he cut off the hands and feet of the golden figure. As a result, the hands and feet of King Sathapaha in the cave also fell off.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南天竺国 - Refers to South India, a region known for its rich cultural and historical background.
  • 郁金柘 - Refers to saffron and lac, which are natural dyes used in traditional Indian textiles.
  • 象马兵 - Refers to an army consisting of elephants and horses, a common military formation in ancient India.
  • 地窟 - Refers to an underground cave, often used for hiding or storing valuables.

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - The king, the main character of the story.
  • 商者 - The merchant, who provides information to the king.
  • 南天竺国娑陀婆恨王 - King Sathapaha of South India.
  • 郁金柘 - Saffron and lac, natural dyes used in traditional Indian textiles.
  • 象马兵 - An army consisting of elephants and horses.
  • 地窟 - An underground cave.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 齊郡接歷山上有古鐵鎖 - On Mount Li in Qi Prefecture, there is an ancient iron chain. '接歷山' indicates the location of the iron chain.
  • 大如人臂 - As thick as a human arm. A straightforward comparison emphasizing the size of the iron chain.
  • 繞其峰再浃 - Winding around its peak twice. '浃' means 'to wrap around' or 'to encircle.'
  • 海中山 - Mountain in the sea. Refers to an island or mountain that was originally in the sea.
  • 山神好移 - The mountain god likes to move. Suggests that the mountain has a tendency to move or shift.
  • 故海神锁之 - Therefore, the sea god locked it. Implies that the sea god took action to prevent the mountain from moving.
  • 挽锁断 - When the chain was pulled, it broke. '挽' means 'to pull' or 'to draw.'
  • 飞来于此矣 - It flew here. Suggests a sudden and dramatic movement of the mountain.
  • 太原郡东有崖山 - East of Taiyuan Prefecture, there is a cliff mountain. '崖山' means 'cliff mountain.'
  • 天旱 - During a drought. Sets the context for the following actions.
  • 土人常烧此山以求雨 - The locals often burn this mountain to祈雨. '土人' means 'locals' or 'people of the land.'
  • 俗传崖山神娶河伯女 - According to local legend, the cliff mountain god married the daughter of the river god. '俗传' means 'according to local legend.'
  • 故河伯见火，必降雨救之 - Therefore, when the river god sees the fire, he must rain to save it. Explains the reason for the rain.
  • 今山上多生水草 - Now there are many aquatic plants on the mountain. Describes the current state of the mountain.
  • 华不注泉 - The Huabu Spring. '华不注' is a specific name for a spring.
  • 齐顷公取水处 - The place where Duke Qi of the State of Qi fetched water. '齐顷公' refers to a historical figure.
  • 方圆百余步 - About 100 steps in diameter. Describes the size of the spring area.
  • 北齐时 - During the Northern Qi dynasty. '北齐' is a specific historical period.
  • 有人以绳千尺沉石试之不穷 - Someone used a thousand-foot rope to sink a stone and found it endless. '沉石' means 'sink a stone.'
  • 石出，赤如血 - When the stone came out, it was red like blood. Describes the appearance of the stone.
  • 其人不久坐事死 - The person did not live long after that and died due to some matter. '坐事' means 'due to some matter' or 'because of some crime.'
  • 荆州永丰县东乡里有卧石一 - In the eastern village of Yongfeng County, Jingzhou, there is a lying stone. '卧石' means 'lying stone.'
  • 长九尺六寸 - Nine feet six inches long. Describes the length of the stone.
  • 其形似人体 - Its shape resembles a human body. Describes the appearance of the stone.
  • 青黄隐起，状若雕刻 - Green and yellow colors rise up, like carved. Describes the stone's appearance in detail.
  • 境若旱，便齐手（一作祭，无“齐”字）而举之 - If there is a drought in the area, they will raise their hands (or perform a ritual) and lift it. '齐手' means 'raise their hands,' and '祭' means 'perform a ritual.'
  • 小举小雨，大举大雨 - A small lift brings a small rain, a large lift brings a large rain. Describes the effect of lifting the stone.
  • 相传此石忽见于此，本长九尺，今加六寸矣 - According to legend, this stone suddenly appeared here. Originally it was nine feet long, but now it is six inches longer. Explains the change in the stone's length.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乃令具舟 - 乃: then or thereupon; 令具舟: ordered to prepare a boat; indicates immediate action taken by the king.
  • 烦驸马一谒海龙王 - 烦: trouble or bother; 驸马: high-ranking official or noble; 谒: to visit or pay respects; polite request for the official to visit the sea dragon king.
  • 但言东海第三汊第十岛长须国有难求救 - 但言: just say or merely state; 有难求救: in trouble and seeking help; instructs the official to convey a specific message.
  • 我国绝微 - 绝微: extremely small or very insignificant; emphasizes the kingdom's status.
  • 须再三言之 - 须: must or need; 再三言之: to say it repeatedly or to emphasize it; indicates urgency and importance of the message.
  • 涕泣执手而别 - 涕泣: tears or crying; 执手: holding hands; describes a poignant farewell.
  • 瞬息至岸 - 瞬息: in a moment or instantly; 至岸: to reach the shore; emphasizes the speed of the journey.
  • 岸沙悉七宝 - 悉: all or entirely; 七宝: seven precious materials; describes the shore as being entirely made of valuable materials.
  • 人皆衣冠长大 - 皆: all or every; 衣冠: clothes and hats; 长大: tall or mature; describes the people as well-dressed and tall.
  • 访其来意 - 访: to inquire or to ask; 来意: intention or purpose; indicates the dragon king's inquiry into the official's purpose.
  • 龙宫状如佛寺所图天宫 - 状如: looks like; 佛寺: Buddhist temple; 所图天宫: heavenly palace depicted in the temple; describes the dragon palace as resembling a heavenly palace depicted in Buddhist temples.
  • 光明迭激，目不能视 - 光明: light; 迭激: alternating or flashing; 目不能视: cannot be seen; describes the intense and dazzling light.
  • 龙王降阶迎士人 - 降阶: descend from the steps; 迎: to greet; describes the dragon king descending to greet the official.
  • 齐级升殿 - 齐级: together on the steps; 升殿: to ascend to the hall; describes the official and the dragon king ascending together.
  • 龙王即令速勘 - 即: immediately; 令: order; 速勘: to quickly investigate; describes the dragon king's immediate order for a quick investigation.
  • 良久，一人自外白曰 - 良久: after a long time; 自外: from outside; 白曰: said; describes someone speaking after a long time.
  • 此岛虾合供大王此月食料 - 此岛: this island; 虾: shrimp; 合: should; 供: supply; 大王: great king; 此月食料: food for this month; describes the shrimp as the food supply for the month.
  • 前日已追到 - 前日: the previous day; 已追到: has already been brought; indicates that the shrimp had already been brought.
  • 客固为虾所魅耳 - 客: guest; 固: certainly; 为: by; 虾: shrimp; 所魅: deceived; 耳: only or just; suggests that the guest was deceived by the shrimp.
  • 吾虽为王，所食皆禀天符，不得妄食 - 吾: I; 虽: although; 为王: as king; 所食: what I eat; 皆: all; 禀天符: according to heavenly decree; 不得妄食: cannot eat arbitrarily; explains the dragon king's dietary restrictions.
  • 今为客减食 - 今: now; 为: for; 客: guest; 减食: reduce food; describes the dragon king reducing the food supply for the guest.
  • 乃令引客视之 - 乃: then; 令: order; 引: lead; 客: guest; 视之: to see it; describes the dragon king ordering someone to lead the guest to see something.
  • 见铁锅数十如屋，满中是虾 - 见: see; 铁锅: iron pots; 数十: dozens; 如屋: like houses; 满中是虾: full of shrimp; describes seeing dozens of large iron pots full of shrimp.
  • 有五六头色赤，大如臂，见客跳跃，似求救状 - 有: there are; 五六头: five or six; 色赤: red in color; 大如臂: as big as an arm; 见客跳跃: jumping in front of the guest; 似求救状: appearing to beg for help; describes seeing five or six large red shrimp jumping and appearing to beg for help.
  • 引者曰：“此虾王也。” - 引者: the one leading; 曰: said; 此虾王也: these are the shrimp kings; describes the leader identifying the large shrimp as the shrimp kings.
  • 士人不觉悲泣 - 士人: the official; 不觉: unconsciously; 悲泣: cry in sorrow; describes the official crying in sorrow.
  • 龙王命放虾王一锅 - 龙王: dragon king; 命: order; 放: release; 虾王: shrimp kings; 一锅: one pot; describes the dragon king ordering the release of one pot of shrimp kings.
  • 令二使送客归中国 - 令: order; 二使: two envoys; 送: send; 客: guest; 归中国: return to China; describes the dragon king ordering two envoys to send the guest back to China.
  • 一夕，至登州 - 一夕: one night; 至登州: arrive at Dengzhou; describes arriving at Dengzhou after one night.
  • 回顾二使，乃巨龙也 - 回顾: look back; 二使: two envoys; 乃: turn out to be; 巨龙: great dragons; describes the official looking back and realizing the envoys were great dragons.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王 - Refers to the king or ruler of the kingdom.
  • 驸马 - Refers to a high-ranking official or noble, often a prince or a close relative of the royal family.
  • 海龙王 - Refers to the sea dragon king, a mythical figure in Chinese folklore and mythology.
  • 东海第三汊第十岛 - Refers to a specific location in the East Sea, which is a common setting in Chinese mythology.
  • 长须国 - Refers to a mythical country known for its inhabitants with long beards.
  • 七宝 - Refers to seven precious materials, often associated with wealth and luxury.
  • 龙宫 - Refers to the dragon palace, a mythical place where dragons live.
  • 佛寺所图天宫 - Refers to the heavenly palace depicted in Buddhist temples, symbolizing a place of divine beauty and power.
  • 天符 - Refers to a heavenly decree or divine command, indicating that the dragon king's actions are sanctioned by a higher power.

[201]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天宝初 - Refers to the early years of the Tianbao era during Emperor Xuanzong's reign.
  • 五色玉带 - A belt made of multicolored jade, symbolizing high status or imperial favor.
  • 左藏库 - The imperial treasury where valuable items were stored.
  • 小勃律 - A kingdom in the region of modern-day Gilgit-Baltistan, known for its strategic location.
  • 比常进皆为小勃律所劫 - Regular tributes were being intercepted by the kingdom of Soubul.
  • 欲征之 - The intention to launch a military campaign against them.
  • 群臣多谏 - Many officials advised against the campaign.
  • 独李右座赞成上意 - Only one official, referred to as '李右座' (Li Youzuo), supported the emperor's decision.
  • 天运谋勇可将 - Li Youzuo's claim that Wutian Wuyong was capable of leading the campaign.
  • 屠城 - To massacre the inhabitants of a city.
  • 虏三千人及其珠玑而还 - Captured 3,000 people along with their jewels and returned.
  • 将军无义，不祥 - The statement made by a fortune-teller in Soubul, indicating that the general lacked righteousness and was bringing bad luck.
  • 小海涨涌 - The sea swelled up violently.
  • 四万人一时冻死 - Four thousand soldiers froze to death at once.
  • 唯蕃汉各一人得还 - Only one foreigner and one Han Chinese soldier survived.
  • 玄宗大惊异 - Emperor Xuanzong was greatly shocked and surprised.
  • 中使随二人验之 - An imperial messenger followed the survivors to verify the event.
  • 冰犹峥嵘如山 - The ice still stood tall like mountains.
  • 隔冰见兵士尸，立者坐者，莹彻可数 - Through the ice, the bodies of standing and sitting soldiers could be seen clearly.
  • 冰忽稍释，众尸亦不复见 - The ice suddenly began to melt, and the bodies disappeared.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天宝 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, known for its prosperity and later downfall.
  • 五色玉 - Multicolored jade, a symbol of imperial favor and wealth.
  • 左藏库 - The imperial treasury, where valuable items were stored.
  • 小勃律 - A kingdom in the region of modern-day Gilgit-Baltistan, strategically important for trade routes.
  • 李右座 - A high-ranking official who supported the emperor's decision.
  • 王天运 - The general who led the campaign.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong, a ruler known for his early reign of peace and prosperity.
  • 中使 - An imperial messenger sent to verify the event.
  • 小海 - Refers to a body of water, possibly a lake or a sea.
  • 莹彻可数 - Clear and distinct, allowing the bodies to be counted.
  • 冰忽稍释 - The ice suddenly began to melt.
  • 众尸亦不复见 - The bodies disappeared.

  • 左藏库 - The imperial treasury.
  • 小勃律 - A kingdom in the region of modern-day Gilgit-Baltistan.
  • 李右座 - A high-ranking official who supported the emperor's decision.
  • 王天运 - The general who led the campaign.

[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中夜有人面如盘，寅目出于灯下 - Midnight, a face as large as a plate with fierce eyes appeared under the lamp.
  • 了无惧色 - Not the least bit afraid.
  • 徐染翰题其颊 - Slowly took up the brush and inscribed on its cheek.
  • 久戍人偏老，长征马不肥 - A famous couplet meaning 'Those who have been on long military service grow old, while horses from long campaigns do not grow fat.'
  • 暴然分为两扇，空中轮转，声如分蜂 - Suddenly split into two halves, spun in the air, sound like a swarm of bees.
  • 忽合于柳氏首，柳氏碎首，齿着于树 - Suddenly joined on Liu’s head, crushed her skull, her teeth stuck to the tree.
  • 露坐逐凉 - Sat outside in the open to seek coolness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大历中 - During the Da Li era (766–779 CE), a reign period of Emperor Daizong of Tang.
  • 士人 - A scholar or a literati, often used to denote someone of high moral and intellectual standing.
  • 柳氏 - A woman surnamed Liu, possibly a reference to Liu Yizhi, a historical figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭代公 - De Gong, possibly a title or honorific for a person named Guo.
  • 渭南 - Weinan, a place in Shaanxi province.
  • 胡蜂 - Hu feng, a type of wasp.
  • 胡桃 - Hu tao, walnut (but here it's a trick or illusion).
